name: Capsicum Frutescens
contraindications: and cardiovascular disease if using high capsaicin intakes.
dosage: Dietary exposure paper discussed typical capsaicinoid intake around 1–30 mg/day; one small red chili extract study used 200 mg Capsifen providing 4 mg capsaicinoids/day for 28 days. | Dietary chili/capsaicinoids; sustained-release red chili extract; capsaicin/capsinoid supplement products. Topical/intranasal/respiratory studies are separate routes and should not be merged. | Food intake, oral extracts, topical capsaicin, intranasal/inhaled capsaicin, and capsiate/capsinoids are separate evidence lanes.
typical_dosage: Dietary exposure paper discussed typical capsaicinoid intake around 1–30 mg/day; one small red chili extract study used 200 mg Capsifen providing 4 mg capsaicinoids/day for 28 days. | Dietary chili/capsaicinoids; sustained-release red chili extract; capsaicin/capsinoid supplement products. Topical/intranasal/respiratory studies are separate routes and should not be merged. | Food intake, oral extracts, topical capsaicin, intranasal/inhaled capsaicin, and capsiate/capsinoids are separate evidence lanes.
safety: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: Spicy Food and Chili Peppers and Multiple Health Outcomes: Umbrella Review
sources.citation: 11 systematic reviews/meta-analyses; health effects unclear; side effects confirmed; context matters

sources.title: Estimation of Dietary Capsaicinoid Exposure in Korea and Assessment of Its Health Effects
sources.citation: Mean capsaicinoid intake 3.25 mg/day; abdominal pain reported; 30 mg/day may be max tolerable for long-term repeated consumption

sources.title: Capsaicin: A Potential Treatment to Improve Cerebrovascular Function and Cognition
sources.citation: Adequate human cognition/cerebrovascular studies do not exist

claimMap.claim: Capsaicin safety is dose-dependent; typical dietary exposures may be tolerated, but abdominal pain/GI distress can occur and high-dose supplemental use needs caution.
claimMap.notes: Capsaicin safety is dose-dependent; typical dietary exposures may be tolerated, but abdominal pain/GI distress can occur and high-dose supplemental use needs caution.

qualifiers.direction: generally below adverse-effect levels; abdominal pain signal
qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Common exposure 1-30 mg capsaicinoids/day; 30 mg/day may be max tolerable for repeated long-term intake

claimMap.claim: Capsicum/capsaicin should not be over-positioned for broad health claims; benefits are disputed and context-dependent.
claimMap.notes: Capsicum/capsaicin should not be over-positioned for broad health claims; benefits are disputed and context-dependent.

qualifiers.direction: mixed/unclear health effects; side effects confirmed

claimMap.claim: Capsaicin has promising animal/mechanistic cognition data, but adequate human studies on cognition/cerebrovascular outcomes do not exist yet.
claimMap.notes: Capsaicin has promising animal/mechanistic cognition data, but adequate human studies on cognition/cerebrovascular outcomes do not exist yet.
